body.title: [pending] Implement scripts/critic.py — adversarial analysis CLI
body.intention: I intend that critics.py provides a lightweight CLI for experimmentation without the skill infrastructure, encouraging iterative refinement.
body.description:
Create `scripts/critic.py` as a standalone script with identical interface and logic to /critic skill. Usage: `python scripts/critic.py <target> [--refresh] [--output json|text|markdown]`. Implementation: (1) Share core critique logic with /critic skill (extract to common module, import in both); (2) CLI argument parsing for target, --refresh, --output; (3) Call shared logic; (4) Format and print output. This allows experimentation from CLI, from cron/automation, and from build scripts without loading the full skill infrastructure.

**Affected files:** scripts/critic.py (new), lib/critic_core.py (shared with skill)
**Design rules:** none apply (experimental, not in critical path)
**Scope boundary:** Script implementation only; shared logic is separate; testing is separate ticket
**Completion criteria:** (1) Script callable via `python scripts/critic.py <target>`; (2) accepts symbol/module/ticket/text input; (3) --refresh and --output flags work; (4) output formats JSON, text, markdown correctly; (5) same cache as skill (1 hour, same target); (6) help/usage text is clear
**Test plan:** (1) Test all input types (symbol, module, ticket, text); (2) Test all output formats; (3) Test --refresh flag; (4) Verify cache is shared with /critic skill; (5) Run from different directories, verify paths resolved correctly; (6) Spot-check output quality
